title: oxalis illinoensis
intro: Oxalis illinoensis, also known as the yellow woodsorrel, is a perennial wildflower with yellow flowers and a sour taste, belonging to the wood sorrel family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Oxalis illinoensis include its potential to support kidney function, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Oxalis illinoensis can be made into teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as urinary tract infections and skin irritations.
